title: NASA’s TESS Mission Reveals the “Puffiest” Planets Ever Found
summary: NASA has revealed two new “super-puff” planets, giant worlds so light that their density is comparable to cotton candy. Scientists calculate that these Jupiter-sized planets are the “puffiest” worlds ever found.

title: Live Coverage: West Coast Falcon 9 launch to continue expansion of SpaceX’s Starlink network
summary: Liftoff of the Starlink 17-45 mission from Space Launch Complex 4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base in California is scheduled for 8:19 p.m. PDT (11:19 p.m. EDT / 0319 UTC).

title: Analysis finds the exploration programs NASA recently canceled were running way late
summary: "Contract values for these efforts ballooned from nearly $2.8 billion to $5.9 billion."

title: NASA at the Ion: Orion Lessons from Artemis II Shape NASA’s Moon to Mars Path
summary: Seven weeks after the Orion spacecraft returned four astronauts from humanity’s first crewed journey around the Moon since Apollo, Artemis II Orion Vehicle Manager Branelle Rodriguez reflected on the mission’s achievements and how it is shaping NASA’s return to the lunar surface and future missions to Mars.  Introduced by NASA’s Johnson Space Center Acting Director of Business Development and Technology Integration Monte Goforth, Rodriguez spoke at the Ion in […]

summary:

title: NASA to Share Latest Moon Base Mission Progress
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman will host a virtual conversation at 2:30 p.m. EDT, Tuesday, June 30, to share updates to NASA’s plans to build a Moon Base on the lunar surface. title: Shield Space and ClearSpace partner to defend satellites from orbital threats
summary:
British startup Shield Space plans to combine its autonomous satellite operations software with ClearSpace’s in-orbit servicing capabilities to address emerging orbital threats.

title: Roman Telescope Comes to Kennedy
summary: In this June 21, 2026, photo, NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope arrives at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida aboard NASA’s Pegasus barge. After offloading and transportation to the spaceport’s Payload Hazardous Servicing Facility, Roman will undergo processing ahead of launch, targeted no earlier than Sunday, Aug. 30, 2026. title: MDA Space wins contract for Radarsat replenishment satellite
summary:
MDA Space will build a radar imaging satellite for the Canadian Space Agency as the government studies options for a next-generation satellite system.
